a: Monitor In-App Purchase events in real time and learn of unreported external purchase tokens, with server notifications from the App Store.
Specify your secure server’s URL in App Store Connect to receive version 1 notifications.
Specify your secure server’s URL in App Store Connect to receive version 2 notifications.
Receive, parse, and interpret App Store Server Notifications version 1.
Learn about changes to the App Store Server Notifications service.

t: Data types for Advanced Commerce API
a: Objects and data types for transaction that use the Advanced Commerce API.
Configure your server and provide an HTTPS URL to receive notifications about in-app purchase events and unreported external purchase tokens.
App transaction information signed by the App Store, in JSON Web Signature (JWS) Compact Serialization format.
A decoded JSON Web Signature header containing transaction or renewal information.
Subscription renewal information signed by the App Store, in JSON Web Signature (JWS) format.
A decoded payload containing subscription renewal information for an auto-renewable subscription.
Transaction information signed by the App Store, in JSON Web Signature (JWS) Compact Serialization format.
A decoded payload that contains transaction information.
Implement server-side code to receive and parse notification posts.

t: Responding to App Store Server Notifications
a: Send HTTP status codes to indicate the success of a notification post.
Refer to these data types for decoded transaction and renewal information payloads.
The JSON Web Signature (JWS) header parameter that identifies the cryptographic algorithm used to secure the JWS.
A UUID that associates the transaction with a customer on your service.
The unique identifier of an app in the App Store.
The object that contains the app metadata and signed app transaction information.
The unique identifier of the app download transaction.
The identifier of the product that renews at the next billing period.
The renewal status for an auto-renewable subscription.
The bundle identifier of an app.
The version of the build that identifies an iteration of the bundle.
The customer-provided reason for a refund request.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price of the product.
The payload data that contains app metadata and the signed renewal and transaction information.
An array of win-back offer identifiers that a customer is eligible to redeem, which sorts the identifiers to present the better offers first.
The server environment, either sandbox or production.
The reason an auto-renewable subscription expired.
The UNIX time, in milliseconds, an auto-renewable subscription purchase expires or renews.
The field of an external purchase token that uniquely identifies the token.
The payload data that contains an external purchase token.
The count of subscriptions that fail to receive a subscription-renewal-date extension.
The time when the billing grace period for a subscription renewal expires.
A string that describes whether the transaction was purchased by the customer, or is available to them through Family Sharing.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the App Store is attempting to automatically renew a subscription that expired due to a billing issue.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the customer upgraded to another subscription.
The type that describes the In-App Purchase or external purchase event for which the App Store sends the version 2 notification.
A unique identifier for the notification.
The type that describes the in-app purchase event for which the App Store sends the version 1 notification.
The payment mode for a discount offer on an In-App Purchase.
The string identifier of an offer that you create in App Store Connect.
The duration of the offer.
The type of offer.
The purchase date of the transaction associated with the original transaction identifier.
The original transaction identifier of a purchase.
The original transaction identifer of a subscription before migration.
The price, in milliunits, of the In-App Purchase that the system records in the transaction.
The status that indicates whether an auto-renewable subscription is subject to a price increase.
The product identifier of the In-App Purchase.
The time that the App Store charged the customer’s account for a purchase, a restored product, a subscription, or a subscription renewal after a lapse.
The number of purchased consumable products.
The earliest start date of a subscription in a series of auto-renewable subscription purchases that ignores all lapses of paid service shorter than 60 days.
The UNIX time, in milliseconds, when the most recent auto-renewable subscription purchase expires.
The renewal price, in milliunits, of the auto-renewable subscription that renews at the next billing period.
A string that contains a unique identifier for a subscription-renewal-date extension request.
The response body containing JSON data that the App Store sends in a version 1 server notification.
The response body the App Store sends in a version 2 server notification.
A decoded payload that contains the version 2 notification data.
The UNIX time, in milliseconds, that the App Store refunded the transaction or revoked it from Family Sharing.
The percentage, in milliunits, of the transaction that the App Store has refunded or revoked.
The reason for a revoked or refunded transaction.
The type of the refund or revocation that applies to the transaction.
The UNIX time, in milliseconds, that the App Store signed the JSON Web Signature data.
A cryptographically signed payload, in JSON Web Signature (JWS) format, that contains the response body for a version 2 notification.
The status of an auto-renewable subscription at the time the App Store signs the notification.
The three-letter code that represents the country or region associated with the App Store storefront for the purchase.
The three-letter code that represents the country or region associated with the App Store storefront.
A list of storefront country codes for limiting the storefronts for a subscription-renewal-date extension.
An Apple-defined value that uniquely identifies an App Store storefront.
The identifier of the subscription group that the subscription belongs to.
A string that provides details about select notification types in version 2.
The count of subscriptions that successfully receive a subscription-renewal-date extension.
The payload data for a subscription-renewal-date extension notification.
The field of an external purchase token that contains the UNIX date, in milliseconds, when the system created the token.
The field of a custom link token that contains the UNIX date, in milliseconds, when the token expires.
The type of an external purchase custom link token.
The unique identifier for a transaction, such as an In-App Purchase, restored purchase, or subscription renewal.
The cause of a purchase transaction, which indicates whether it’s a customer’s purchase or a renewal for an auto-renewable subscription that the system initiates.
The product type of the In-App Purchase.
An object that contains information about the most recent in-app purchase transactions for the app.
An object that contains information about the latest in-app subscription transaction.
An array of elements that refers to open auto-renewable subscription renewals or ones that failed in the past.
A string that indicates the notification’s App Store Server Notifications version number.
The unique identifier of subscription purchase events across devices, including subscription renewals.
The JSON Web Signature (JWS) header parameter that contains the certificate chain that corresponds to the key used to digitally sign the JWS.
